fields.description:
# Tier-1 opportunistic detached drain + `rebar enrich` CLI

## Why
The feature must run async across server/PC/Mac/cloud with ZERO client setup — no daemon, broker, or scheduler. The proven pattern (git gc --auto, npm update-notifier) piggybacks a cheap, detached drain on ordinary invocations. rebar ships the POSIX half of this already for async push (`src/rebar/_store/push.py:108`).

## What
- A `maybe_drain()` gate on ordinary invocations: a cheap "is anything soaked+unclaimed?" check that no-ops in the common case, else DETACHES the enrichment to a child that outlives the command.
- **Detach, cross-platform (DERISKED at API level):** POSIX reuses push.py's approach (`start_new_session=True`, `close_fds=True`) + its PYTHONPATH bootstrap. push.py has NO Windows path — it calls `start_new_session=True` unconditionally, which on Windows maps to `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` and does NOT orphan-protect. Windows is therefore NEW work, branched on `os.name` (the established codebase pattern; cf. `acli_subprocess.py`): `creationflags=subprocess.DETACHED_PROCESS | subprocess.CREATE_NO_WINDOW` (these constants exist only on Windows — reference them ONLY inside the `os.name == "nt"` branch or the POSIX import AttributeErrors).
- **Observability:** the child configures a `logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler` (maxBytes+backupCount bounded) writing to `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log` (git-ignored) and redirects its own stderr there; stdout/stdin are DEVNULL — drain failures stay diagnosable.
- **Drainer loop (owns DONE + self-healing + growth-bound, moved here from S3):** from `pending_enrichment(now)` PLUS the fallback scan (tickets certified but with absent/stale digest — S3's defense-in-depth), claim one (read-reduce-check-append under `lock.acquire`, per S3), run `enrich` (S1), write `TICKET_DIGEST` (S2), append `DONE_ENRICH`, then `prune` the ticket's queue events so log growth is bounded. **Prune rule (concrete, no "cycle" ambiguity):** keep=1 — retain ONLY the single latest queue event per ticket by HLC (the `DONE_ENRICH` tombstone just written), and drop ALL superseded queue events for that ticket (every prior `ENQUEUE_ENRICH`, `CLAIM_ENRICH`, and older `DONE_ENRICH`). The retained `DONE_ENRICH` means "not pending" to the reducer until a later `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` (a re-certification) supersedes it. This bounds each ticket's queue footprint to one event (mirrors the digest-sidecar `prune(keep=1)`). Process UP TO `DRAIN_BATCH` per run, then exit. Per-item error handling: an `enrich` exception (LLM timeout/rate-limit) releases the claim (lease expiry) and is logged; the batch continues to the next ticket; the failed ticket is re-picked on a later drain.
- **CLI `rebar enrich`:** `--drain` (bounded drain, the detached target), `--once` (claim+process one entry, in-process, tests), `status` (prints JSON `{"pending":int,"claimed":int,"soaking":int}` from the queue reducer). `overlap_drain ∈ {off, async, always}`, DEFAULT `async` (`async` = the opportunistic detached drain of this design; `always` = synchronous inline drain for tests/CI; `off` = no drain). Opt-out is `overlap_drain = "off"` on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`), env `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN` (the ONE canonical name — used in What, Scope, and ACs identically). `overlap_drain` is a STRING enum (`off|async|always`) so it lives on `LLMConfig` (read via `from_env`), NOT `VerifyConfig` (which is bool-only via `_as_bool`).

## Constants (defaults stated)
`DRAIN_BATCH = 5`, `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS = 20` — both on `LLMConfig`.

## maybe_drain injection point (decided — no "or")
Async push is actually triggered inside `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` (`push.push_tickets_branch`, line ~189), so `maybe_drain()` rides the SAME opportunistic seam: called once at the end of the write-command path in `event_append`, immediately after the push, behind the cheap soaked?-check (the check no-ops on the common event so it does not fire per-event-append meaningfully). Single authoritative site; NOT `review_plan`/`claim` individually.

## Dependencies
S1 (enrich op), S2 (digest store), S3/S4 (queue + claim primitive + event types). This story
WIRES them; they are prerequisite sibling stories, not existing code.

**Event-type ownership (resolves the "DONE_ENRICH unregistered" concern):** the three queue
event types — `ENQUEUE_ENRICH`, `CLAIM_ENRICH`, `DONE_ENRICH` — are added to
`event_append.EVENT_TYPES` (write allow-list) and `reducer._version._NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`
by the QUEUE story S4 (`vegan-forgetful-tiger`, `e1f4`), which this story `depends_on`. This
story only **emits** `DONE_ENRICH` (and appends `CLAIM_ENRICH`) via the drainer loop — it does
NOT re-register the types. Because S4 lands below S6 in the stack, the types exist before this
story's writes run; `test_done_enrich_registered` asserts `DONE_ENRICH in event_append.EVENT_TYPES`
as a guard so a missing prerequisite fails loudly rather than raising a StoreError at drain time.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py`; CLI arm in `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`rebar enrich` with `--drain`/`--once`/`status`); **CLI dispatch intercept in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`** — add `if argv and argv[0] == "enrich": return _llm_commands.cmd_enrich(argv[1:], tracker)` alongside the existing `review`/`review-plan`/`verify-completion` intercepts (~line 375-400); without it `rebar enrich` is unroutable. The `maybe_drain` hook in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` (beside `push_tickets_branch`).
- `src/rebar/llm/config.py`: add `overlap_drain` (str enum `off|async|always`, default `async`), `overlap_drain_batch` (int, default 5), `overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms` (int, default 20) as `LLMConfig` fields AND wire them in `from_env()`: `overlap_drain=_llm_str(table, cli, "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN", "overlap_drain", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_DRAIN)` (validate the value is in `{off,async,always}`, else fall back to default), `overlap_drain_batch=_llm_int(..., "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN_BATCH", "overlap_drain_batch", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_DRAIN_BATCH)`, `overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms=_llm_int(..., "REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS", "overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms", DEFAULT_OVERLAP_DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS)`, with the three `DEFAULT_*` module constants.
- OUT: native scheduler (idea Tier-2), CI drain (idea Tier-3).

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py`: `test_once` (one entry end-to-end, FakeRunner); `test_batch_cap` (`DRAIN_BATCH` bound, backlog drains over runs); `test_gate_latency` (maybe_drain no-op < `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS`, no spawn when nothing soaked); `test_lock_held_skip` (silent exit 0, logged); `test_enrich_error_continues` (one enrich() raises → claim released, batch continues); `test_no_key_noop` ([agents]/key absent → clean no-op); `test_opt_out` (`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN=off`); `test_self_heal` (certified-but-stale-digest ticket picked up by fallback scan); `test_windows_detach_kwargs` (os.name branch selects creationflags — mocked); `test_status` (`rebar enrich status` prints JSON `{"pending":int,"claimed":int,"soaking":int}` derived from the queue reducer over a seeded store — exact counts asserted); `test_done_enrich_registered` (`DONE_ENRICH in event_append.EVENT_TYPES` — the S4-prerequisite guard); `test_always_mode` (with `overlap_drain="always"`, a write-path `maybe_drain` runs the drain SYNCHRONOUSLY inline — no detached child — and the entry is processed before the call returns; the CI/test mode); `test_cli_dispatch` (`rebar enrich status` routes through the `_cli/__init__.py` intercept and returns 0); `test_config` (sets `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN_BATCH`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS`; `LLMConfig.from_env()` resolves them; an invalid `overlap_drain` value falls back to the default).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar enrich --drain` processes up to `DRAIN_BATCH` (default 5) soaked+unclaimed entries then exits; backlog drains over successive runs; `pytest ...::test_batch_cap`.
- [ ] `maybe_drain` no-ops with no spawn when nothing soaked, within `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS` (20 ms); detaches a surviving child otherwise; `::{test_gate_latency}`.
- [ ] Concurrent drains don't double-process (lock-held → silent exit 0, logged to `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log`); `::test_lock_held_skip`.
- [ ] A single `enrich` failure releases the claim, logs, and the batch continues; the ticket re-drains later; `::test_enrich_error_continues`. Self-healing fallback picks up cert-without-fresh-digest tickets; `::test_self_heal`.
- [ ] `[agents]`/key absent → clean no-op; `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_DRAIN=off` disables the opportunistic drain (single canonical env name); `::{test_no_key_noop,test_opt_out}`.
- [ ] Detach is `os.name`-branched: POSIX `start_new_session`, Windows `DETACHED_PROCESS|CREATE_NO_WINDOW` (referenced only in the nt branch); `::test_windows_detach_kwargs`. Each processed ticket gets a `DONE_ENRICH` append + a `prune` of its queue events (bounding growth); verified by `::test_batch_cap` observing DONE + a post-drain pending count of 0.
- [ ] `rebar enrich status` prints JSON `{"pending":int,"claimed":int,"soaking":int}` computed from the queue reducer; `::test_status`.
- [ ] `overlap_drain` (`off|async|always`), `overlap_drain_batch`, `overlap_drain_gate_budget_ms` are read from the env by `LLMConfig.from_env()` (invalid `overlap_drain` → default); `DONE_ENRICH` is registered (by S4) before drain writes; `::{test_config,test_done_enrich_registered}`.
- [ ] `overlap_drain="always"` runs the drain synchronously inline (no detached child), processing the entry before the write returns; `::test_always_mode`. `rebar enrich` is routed via the `_cli/__init__.py` dispatch intercept; `::test_cli_dispatch`.
- [ ] The prune step retains exactly the latest queue event per ticket (the `DONE_ENRICH` tombstone), dropping superseded `ENQUEUE_ENRICH`/`CLAIM_ENRICH`/older `DONE_ENRICH` (keep=1, bounded growth); verified by `::test_batch_cap` (post-drain per-ticket queue-event count == 1).
